original_image_tags: Hand-drawn sketch on plain paper. The sketch shows five positions of an object, numbered 1 through 5, connected by dotted lines to indicate a path of movement. Positions 1, 2, and 3 are drawn as ovals. Position 4 is a thin, dark, horizontal ellipse, as if the object is being viewed edge-on. Position 5 is an arc shape with an arrow pointing away. The path shows the object rising in a staggered manner from 1 to 3, then moving horizontally to the position where it rolls (4), before disappearing along a curved, rolling path at (5).

elements.description: A hand-drawn diagram depicting the path of an object, with five numbered positions (1 through 5). Positions 1, 2, and 3 are drawn as small circles, position 4 as a small horizontal dash, and position 5 as a small arrow. A dashed line connects the positions. The diagram is labeled 'Chart to which reference is made on page six, statement of David N. Johnson'.

judge.reason: Both agree on the core elements of a hand-drawn diagram with five numbered positions and a connecting line, but they disagree on the specific shapes (ovals/circles vs circles/dash/arrow) and one adds a label while the other does not, without a direct verbatim text contradiction.
